title: spergularia marina var. urbica
intro: Spergularia marina var. urbica, also known as Sea Sandwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its white or pink flowers and ability to thrive in coastal environments. Traditionally, Sea Sandwort has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sea Sandwort include its ability to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, making it a potential remedy for conditions like asthma and allergies. Sea Sandwort can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic properties.
